text.title: Spec Examples Basic Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Spec Examples Basic. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Maintain exactly four basic examples: CRUD feature, integration, internal tool, polish.
rule.rationale: Four archetypes cover ~70% of solo specs.

rule.statement: Every example fully filled in; no placeholders.
Adapted spec records archetype = one of {crud, integration, internal-tool, polish}.
Every section adapted OR marked 'kept from example'.
Examples reviewed quarterly against current stack.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-spec-examples-basic.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
